algorithms.title: Rabin-Karp String Search
algorithms.description: Rabin-Karp uses rolling hashing to search for a pattern in a text in O(N+M) average time. Instead of comparing characters one by one at each position, it computes a hash of each window of length M and only does a full string comparison when hashes match. Excellent for multiple pattern search.
algorithms.intuition: Hashing a window takes O(M) time, but UPDATING the hash when sliding the window by one character takes O(1) — the 'rolling hash'. Remove the contribution of the leftmost character, shift left, add the new rightmost character. Compare hashes first; only compare strings on hash collision to handle false positives.
algorithms.steps: [{"step":1,"description":"Compute hash of pattern and first window of text (length M)."},{"step":2,"description":"Slide window right: remove leftmost char contribution, add new rightmost char."},{"step":3,"description":"If hash(window) == hash(pattern): verify with full string comparison (handle collisions)."},{"step":4,"description":"Report match position on verification success."}]
algorithms.timeComplexity: O(N+M) average, O(NM) worst (many hash collisions)

useCases.title: Substring matching

variants.title: Multi-pattern Rabin-Karp
variants.desc: Store all pattern hashes in a HashSet; check each window hash against the set

variants.title: 2D Rabin-Karp
variants.desc: Extend rolling hash to 2D for pattern matching in matrices

algorithms.title: Z-Algorithm
algorithms.description: The Z-algorithm computes for each position i in a string the length of the longest substring starting at i that matches a prefix of the string. Z[0] = length of string (by convention), Z[i] = length of match with prefix. Used for pattern matching in O(N+M): build Z on pattern#text, then Z[i] = M means pattern found at i-M-1.
algorithms.intuition: Maintain a window [L, R] representing the rightmost Z-interval found so far. For position i inside [L,R], Z[i] >= min(Z[i-L], R-i+1) — we can reuse previously computed information. Extend from this known lower bound instead of starting from scratch.
algorithms.steps: [{"step":1,"description":"Build Z array for string S of length N."},{"step":2,"description":"Maintain L, R = 0. For each i from 1 to N-1:"},{"step":3,"description":"If i <= R, Z[i] = min(Z[i-L], R-i+1) as a starting point."},{"step":4,"description":"Try to extend: while i+Z[i] < N and S[Z[i]] == S[i+Z[i]], increment Z[i]."},{"step":5,"description":"If i+Z[i]-1 > R, update L=i, R=i+Z[i]-1."}]

useCases.title: Pattern matching
useCases.desc: Concatenate pattern + separator + text; Z values equal to pattern length are matches

useCases.title: Lexicographically smallest rotation
useCases.desc: Find canonical form of a string

variants.title: KMP (Knuth-Morris-Pratt)
variants.desc: Similar O(N+M) matching using failure function instead of Z array

variants.title: Suffix Array
